potential_causes: Cause is unknown; this melanoma with Spitzoid morphology is defined by kinase gene fusions (for example ALK, ROS1, NTRK1, BRAF) rather than UV damage, shows a low UV mutational burden, and has no established environmental or hereditary etiologic factor.
source: WHO Classification of Skin Tumours 5th ed (2023); kinase fusions ROS1/ALK/NTRK1/RET/BRAF (Lab Invest 2017)

name: Trichilemmal carcinoma
potential_causes: Generally arises on chronically sun-damaged skin of elderly patients, implicating cumulative UV exposure; it is otherwise sporadic with no established hereditary cause and follows an indolent course.
source: WHO Classification of Skin Tumours 5th ed (2023); Orphanet

name: Uveal melanoma
aliases: Ocular melanoma; Choroidal melanoma; intraocular melanoma
potential_causes: Largely of unknown cause and, unlike cutaneous melanoma, not clearly linked to UV exposure; established risk factors are light iris/skin color, oculodermal melanocytosis (nevus of Ota), and BAP1 tumor predisposition syndrome (germline BAP1), with driver mutations in GNAQ/GNA11.

name: Well-differentiated neuroendocrine tumor of the pancreas
aliases: pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor; islet cell tumor; PanNET
potential_causes: Most are sporadic with no established cause; a minority arise in hereditary syndromes, notably MEN1 (germline MEN1), von Hippel-Lindau disease (VHL), neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1), and tuberous sclerosis (TSC1/TSC2).
source: WHO Classification of Endocrine and Neuroendocrine Tumours 5th ed; MEN1-associated; incidence <1 per 100,000 (RARECAREnet)

name: Adult fibrosarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically as a diagnosis of exclusion with no defining genetic alteration and no consistent inherited or environmental cause; prior therapeutic radiation can rarely precede fibrosarcoma at an irradiated site.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining PAX3::FOXO1 or PAX7::FOXO1 fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause; a minority of rhabdomyosarcomas occur in cancer predisposition syndromes such as Li-Fraumeni (germline TP53), DICER1, and neurofibromatosis type 1.

name: Alveolar soft part sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining ASPSCR1::TFE3 fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.

name: Angiomatoid fibrous histiocytoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining EWSR1::CREB1 (or EWSR1::ATF1 / FUS::ATF1) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Angiosarcoma
potential_causes: Several established etiologic factors exist: prior therapeutic radiation (including secondary breast angiosarcoma), chronic lymphedema (Stewart-Treves syndrome after mastectomy), and industrial vinyl chloride or Thorotrast exposure for hepatic angiosarcoma; ultraviolet exposure is linked to cutaneous head and neck angiosarcoma.

name: Biphenotypic sinonasal sarcoma
aliases: Low-grade sinonasal sarcoma with neural and myogenic features
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically in the sinonasal tract. The defining PAX3::MAML3 (or other PAX3) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: CIC-rearranged sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining CIC::DUX4 (or other CIC) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Clear cell sarcoma of soft tissue
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining EWSR1::ATF1 (or EWSR1::CREB1) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Composite hemangioendothelioma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically as a rare low-grade vascular tumor with no recognized inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Dedifferentiated liposarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The characteristic MDM2/CDK4 amplification is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Desmoid-type fibromatosis
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic driven by somatic CTNNB1 (beta-catenin) mutation, but a recognized subset arises in familial adenomatous polyposis (germline APC mutation, Gardner syndrome); pregnancy, prior trauma, and surgery are reported associations.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Desmoplastic small round cell tumor
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining EWSR1::WT1 fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.

name: Ectomesenchymoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically as a rare pediatric tumor with rhabdomyoblastic and neuronal elements and no recognized inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma
potential_causes: Mostly sporadic with no established cause, but this is the rhabdomyosarcoma subtype most strongly linked to germline cancer predisposition syndromes, including Li-Fraumeni (germline TP53), DICER1 syndrome, Costello syndrome (HRAS), neurofibromatosis type 1, and Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome.

name: Epithelioid hemangioendothelioma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining WWTR1::CAMTA1 (or YAP1::TFE3) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.

name: Epithelioid inflammatory myofibroblastic sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. It is driven by somatic ALK gene rearrangement (often RANBP2::ALK), which is an acquired event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Epithelioid sarcoma
aliases: Proximal-type epithelioid sarcoma; Distal-type epithelioid sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The characteristic biallelic somatic inactivation of SMARCB1 (INI1) is an acquired event, not an inherited or environmental cause.

name: Extrarenal rhabdoid tumor
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic driven by biallelic somatic SMARCB1 (INI1) inactivation, but a substantial subset arises in the rhabdoid tumor predisposition syndrome from a germline SMARCB1 (or rarely SMARCA4) mutation.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Extraskeletal Ewing sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining EWSR1::FLI1 (or EWSR1::ERG) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Extraskeletal myxoid chondrosarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining EWSR1::NR4A3 (or TAF15::NR4A3) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.

name: Extraskeletal osteosarcoma
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with complex genomic changes and no established cause, but prior therapeutic radiation to the site is a recognized risk factor for a subset of extraskeletal osteosarcomas.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Infantile fibrosarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically in infants. The defining ETV6::NTRK3 fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. It is driven by somatic kinase gene rearrangements (most often ALK, also ROS1, NTRK, PDGFRB), which are acquired events, not an inherited or environmental cause.

name: Intimal sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically within large blood vessels or the heart, with recurrent somatic MDM2 amplification that is acquired, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Kaposi sarcoma
potential_causes: Caused by human herpesvirus 8 (HHV-8 / KSHV) infection, which is necessary for all clinical forms; disease develops in the setting of immunosuppression, most notably HIV coinfection (amplifying risk several hundred to thousand fold) and iatrogenic immunosuppression after solid organ transplant.

name: Leiomyosarcoma
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause, but a subset arises in hereditary cancer syndromes, chiefly Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53) and hereditary retinoblastoma (germline RB1); prior therapeutic radiation is also a recognized risk factor for radiation-associated leiomyosarcoma.

name: Low-grade fibromyxoid sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining FUS::CREB3L2 (or FUS::CREB3L1) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Malignant glomus tumor
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause, though multiple/familial glomus tumors can occur with germline glomulin (GLMN) mutation; malignant transformation itself has no recognized inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Malignant granular cell tumor
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically, usually as a rare malignant counterpart of the Schwann cell derived granular cell tumor, with no recognized inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Malignant myoepithelioma of soft tissue
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically, frequently with somatic EWSR1 (or FUS) gene rearrangement, which is an acquired event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Malignant ossifying fibromyxoid tumor
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically with recurrent somatic PHF1 gene rearrangement that is acquired, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Malignant perivascular epithelioid cell tumor
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause, but a subset arises in the tuberous sclerosis complex (germline TSC1 or TSC2 mutation), consistent with the recurrent somatic TSC pathway alterations seen in these tumors.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Malignant phosphaturic mesenchymal tumor
aliases: Malignant phosphaturic mesenchymal tumor mixed connective tissue variant
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. Most harbor a somatic FN1::FGFR1 (or FN1::FGF1) fusion driving FGF23 secretion, which is an acquired event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Malignant solitary fibrous tumor
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining NAB2::STAT6 fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Malignant tenosynovial giant cell tumor
aliases: Malignant giant cell tumor of tendon sheath
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically as a rare malignant counterpart of tenosynovial giant cell tumor, typically with somatic CSF1 rearrangement, which is an acquired event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Malignant Triton tumor
potential_causes: This is a malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor with rhabdomyoblastic differentiation; about half of cases arise in patients with neurofibromatosis type 1 (germline NF1), and prior therapeutic radiation is also an established risk factor.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Myxofibrosarcoma
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause and complex genomic changes; a recognized subset is radiation-associated, arising years after therapeutic radiation to the region.

name: Myxoid liposarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining FUS::DDIT3 (or EWSR1::DDIT3) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.

name: Myxoid pleomorphic liposarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically as a rare aggressive liposarcoma of children and young adults, with reported occurrence in Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53) but most cases having no identified inherited cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Myxoinflammatory fibroblastic sarcoma
aliases: MIFS; Inflammatory myxohyaline tumor; Acral myxoinflammatory fibroblastic sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically, typically in the distal extremities, with recurrent somatic TGFBR3/MGEA5 rearrangement or BRAF alteration that is acquired, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: NTRK-rearranged spindle cell neoplasm
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining NTRK1/2/3 gene rearrangement is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Papillary intralymphatic angioendothelioma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically (Dabska tumor) as a rare low-grade vascular tumor with no recognized inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Pleomorphic liposarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically with complex genomic changes and no defining fusion, and no consistent inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Pleomorphic rhabdomyosarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically in adults with complex genomic changes and no defining fusion, and no consistent inherited or environmental cause.

name: Pseudomyogenic hemangioendothelioma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining SERPINE1::FOSB (or ACTB::FOSB) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Retiform hemangioendothelioma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically as a rare low-grade vascular tumor with no recognized inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Round cell sarcoma with EWSR1-non-ETS fusion
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining EWSR1 (or FUS) fusion to a non-ETS partner such as NFATC2 or PATZ1 is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Sarcoma with BCOR genetic alteration
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining BCOR alteration (BCOR::CCNB3 fusion or BCOR internal tandem duplication) is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Sclerosing epithelioid fibrosarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The recurrent EWSR1::CREB3L1 (or FUS::CREB3L2) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Spindle cell/sclerosing rhabdomyosarcoma
key_gene_or_marker: MYOD1 p.L122R; VGLL2/NCOA2 fusions (infantile); TFCP2/MEIS1 fusions
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. It is driven by somatic events (for example VGLL2/NCOA2 fusions in infants or MYOD1 mutation in adults), which are acquired, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Synovial sarcoma
aliases: Monophasic synovial sarcoma; Biphasic synovial sarcoma; Poorly differentiated synovial sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining SS18::SSX gene fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.

name: Undifferentiated pleomorphic sarcoma
aliases: Malignant fibrous histiocytoma; UPS; Undifferentiated sarcoma
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause, but a recognized subset is radiation-associated, arising years after therapeutic radiation to a prior site; occurrence in Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53) is also reported.

name: Undifferentiated round cell sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically as an undifferentiated small round cell sarcoma lacking a defining recurrent alteration and with no consistent inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Undifferentiated spindle cell sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically as a diagnosis of exclusion with no defining alteration and no consistent inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet

name: Well-differentiated liposarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The characteristic MDM2/CDK4 amplification on supernumerary ring or giant marker chromosomes is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed Soft Tissue & Bone; Orphanet; NCI

name: Adamantinoma of long bones
potential_causes: A rare low-grade biphasic bone tumor of the tibia/fibula that shows a well-recognized clinicopathologic relationship with osteofibrous dysplasia (regarded as part of a spectrum/possible precursor), but it has no established environmental exposure or hereditary predisposition.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Angiosarcoma of bone
potential_causes: Predominantly sporadic; a subset is secondary to prior radiation therapy or arises in bone infarcts or at sites of chronic injury, but no strong hereditary predisposition is established for the bone form.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor
potential_causes: Germline SMARCB1 (or rarely SMARCA4) loss-of-function variants in rhabdoid tumor predisposition syndrome underlie a substantial minority of cases; the remainder are sporadic from biallelic somatic SMARCB1 inactivation, with no established environmental cause.

name: Clear cell chondrosarcoma
potential_causes: A rare low-grade chondrosarcoma of the epiphyses that is sporadic; no established environmental cause or hereditary predisposition is defined.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: CNS embryonal tumor, not otherwise specified
aliases: CNS embryonal tumour NOS; CNS embryonal tumor NEC
potential_causes: A heterogeneous group of primitive CNS embryonal tumors lacking a defining molecular alteration; it is sporadic with no established environmental cause, though rare cases may occur with cancer predisposition syndromes.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: CNS neuroblastoma, FOXR2-activated
potential_causes: A CNS embryonal tumor defined by somatic FOXR2 activation; it is sporadic with no established hereditary predisposition or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: CNS tumor with BCOR internal tandem duplication
aliases: CNS tumour with BCOR internal tandem duplication
potential_causes: A CNS embryonal tumor defined by a somatic BCOR internal tandem duplication; it is sporadic with no established hereditary predisposition or environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: Conventional chondrosarcoma
potential_causes: Most are sporadic and arise de novo, but a subset develops from benign precursor lesions, including secondary transformation of enchondromas (Ollier disease and Maffucci syndrome, driven by IDH1/IDH2 mutations) or osteochondromas (multiple osteochondromas, EXT1/EXT2).
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Conventional chordoma
potential_causes: Arises from persistent notochordal remnants along the axial skeleton (clivus, spine, sacrum), often from benign notochordal cell tumors; rare familial chordoma is caused by germline duplication of the T (TBXT/brachyury) gene, and tuberous sclerosis is a rare predisposition.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020); RARECAREnet

name: Conventional osteosarcoma
aliases: Conventional central osteosarcoma; High-grade central osteosarcoma
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic, but established risk factors include hereditary retinoblastoma (germline RB1), Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53), Rothmund-Thomson and other RECQL-helicase syndromes, prior therapeutic radiation, and rapid bone growth in adolescence.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Dedifferentiated adamantinoma
potential_causes: Arises from high-grade transformation of a conventional adamantinoma of long bones; it is sporadic with no established environmental or hereditary cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Dedifferentiated chondrosarcoma
potential_causes: Arises when a low-grade cartilaginous tumor (often a preexisting or concurrent conventional chondrosarcoma or enchondroma) transforms to a high-grade nonchondroid sarcoma; it shares enchondromatosis-associated IDH mutation etiology but is otherwise sporadic.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Dedifferentiated chordoma
potential_causes: Arises when a conventional chordoma (from notochordal remnants) transforms into a high-grade sarcomatous component; it is sporadic and shares the notochordal origin of conventional chordoma, sometimes after prior treatment.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Embryonal tumor with multilayered rosettes
aliases: ETMR; Embryonal tumour with multilayered rosettes, C19MC-altered
potential_causes: Most cases are driven by amplification of the C19MC microRNA cluster (chr19q13.42), a somatic event; a minority arise in the context of germline DICER1 pathogenic variants (DICER1 syndrome), and it is otherwise sporadic with no environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: Ewing sarcoma
aliases: Ewing sarcoma of bone; Ewing tumor
potential_causes: Sporadic with no established environmental or hereditary cause; defined by a somatic EWSR1::FLI1 (or related ETS) fusion that is an oncogenic driver rather than an inherited risk factor, and incidence is notably higher in people of European ancestry than African or Asian ancestry.

name: Fibrosarcoma of bone
potential_causes: A now-rare diagnosis of exclusion; most are sporadic primary bone tumors, though a subset is secondary to prior radiation, Paget disease of bone, or bone infarct, similar to other high-grade bone sarcomas.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Ganglioneuroblastoma
potential_causes: Predominantly sporadic peripheral neuroblastic tumor; a minority relate to the same neuroblastoma predisposition genes (germline ALK or PHOX2B), and no established environmental cause exists.

name: High-grade surface osteosarcoma
potential_causes: A rare high-grade osteosarcoma arising on the bone surface; it is generally sporadic within the broader osteosarcoma risk context (RB1, TP53, radiation) and has no established subtype-specific cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Leiomyosarcoma of bone
potential_causes: A rare primary smooth muscle sarcoma of bone that is largely sporadic; a subset is radiation-associated, and rare cases occur in Li-Fraumeni syndrome or in EBV-associated smooth muscle tumors of immunosuppressed patients, but no defined common cause exists.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Low-grade central osteosarcoma
potential_causes: A rare intramedullary low-grade osteosarcoma characterized by MDM2 amplification; it is sporadic with no established environmental cause or defined hereditary predisposition.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Malignant giant cell tumor of bone
aliases: Malignancy in giant cell tumor of bone
potential_causes: Usually arises from malignant transformation of a conventional (H3F3A-mutant) giant cell tumor of bone, either as primary malignancy or, importantly, secondary malignancy after radiotherapy of a giant cell tumor; otherwise sporadic with no environmental cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Malignant rhabdoid tumor
aliases: Extracranial malignant rhabdoid tumour; Rhabdoid tumor of soft tissue
potential_causes: Germline SMARCB1 (rarely SMARCA4) mutations in rhabdoid tumor predisposition syndrome cause a notable fraction of extracranial cases; the rest are sporadic from biallelic somatic SMARCB1 loss, with no environmental cause established.

name: Medulloblastoma, non-WNT/non-SHH
potential_causes: Comprises group 3 and group 4 medulloblastoma, which are largely sporadic with no established hereditary syndrome or environmental cause; driven by somatic events such as MYC amplification and structural chromosomal changes.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: Medulloblastoma, SHH-activated and TP53-mutant
potential_causes: This aggressive subgroup is strongly enriched for germline TP53 mutations (Li-Fraumeni syndrome), and some cases carry germline PTCH1 or SUFU variants; the TP53 mutation drives the SHH-activated phenotype.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: Medulloblastoma, SHH-activated and TP53-wildtype
potential_causes: SHH-pathway medulloblastoma is associated with germline predisposition, notably Gorlin (nevoid basal cell carcinoma) syndrome from PTCH1 or SUFU mutations, and germline DICER1 or PTCH1 variants; many cases are still sporadic.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: Medulloblastoma, WNT-activated
potential_causes: Mostly sporadic, but the WNT subgroup is enriched for germline APC mutations (familial adenomatous polyposis / Turcot syndrome); somatic CTNNB1 mutation is the usual driver in sporadic cases.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: Medulloepithelioma
potential_causes: A rare embryonal CNS or intraocular tumor recapitulating primitive neural tube; most are sporadic, though intraocular (ciliary body) medulloepithelioma is associated with germline DICER1 pathogenic variants (DICER1 syndrome).
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Central Nervous System, 2021)

name: Mesenchymal chondrosarcoma
potential_causes: A rare aggressive chondrosarcoma defined by a somatic HEY1::NCOA2 fusion; it is sporadic with no established environmental exposure or hereditary predisposition.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Neuroblastoma
potential_causes: Mostly sporadic, but familial neuroblastoma is driven by germline ALK activating mutations or PHOX2B mutations, and it also arises in predisposition syndromes (e.g., congenital central hypoventilation and Hirschsprung disease with PHOX2B); no strong environmental cause is established.

name: Parosteal osteosarcoma
potential_causes: A low-grade surface osteosarcoma defined by MDM2/CDK4 amplification (supernumerary ring chromosomes); it is sporadic with no established environmental or hereditary cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Periosteal chondrosarcoma
potential_causes: A rare surface chondrosarcoma that is generally sporadic; no established environmental or hereditary cause is defined, though it belongs to the cartilaginous tumor spectrum that can associate with enchondromatosis.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Periosteal osteosarcoma
potential_causes: An intermediate-grade chondroblastic surface osteosarcoma that is sporadic; no established environmental exposure or hereditary predisposition is defined for this subtype.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Pineoblastoma
aliases: Pineoblastoma, molecularly defined; Pineal parenchymal tumor of poor differentiation
potential_causes: Predisposition includes germline RB1 mutation (trilateral retinoblastoma) and germline DICER1 pathogenic variants (DICER1 syndrome), with additional familial cases linked to RB1 and DICER1 pathway loss; most cases are otherwise sporadic.

name: Poorly differentiated chordoma
potential_causes: A rare aggressive chordoma variant defined by SMARCB1 (INI1) loss, arising from notochordal remnants; it is predominantly sporadic, though rare cases occur in patients with SMARCB1-related predisposition.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Retinoblastoma
potential_causes: Caused by biallelic RB1 inactivation; heritable (bilateral or familial) disease results from a germline RB1 loss-of-function mutation, whereas unilateral sporadic disease arises from two somatic RB1 hits.

name: Secondary osteosarcoma
aliases: Osteosarcoma secondary to Paget disease; Radiation-associated osteosarcoma
potential_causes: By definition arises on a background of a predisposing condition, most commonly prior therapeutic or occupational radiation and Paget disease of bone in older adults, and also from bone infarcts, fibrous dysplasia, or chronic osteomyelitis.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Secondary peripheral chondrosarcoma
potential_causes: Arises from malignant transformation of the cartilage cap of a preexisting osteochondroma, so multiple osteochondromas (hereditary EXT1/EXT2 mutations) is an established predisposing condition; solitary osteochondromas can also transform.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Small cell osteosarcoma
potential_causes: A rare osteosarcoma variant that is generally sporadic; it falls within the broader osteosarcoma risk context (hereditary RB1, Li-Fraumeni TP53, prior radiation) but has no established subtype-specific cause.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Telangiectatic osteosarcoma
potential_causes: A rare high-grade osteosarcoma variant that is generally sporadic but shares the osteosarcoma predisposition context (hereditary RB1 retinoblastoma, Li-Fraumeni TP53, and prior radiation); no distinct environmental cause is established for this subtype.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Undifferentiated high-grade pleomorphic sarcoma of bone
potential_causes: Mostly a sporadic primary high-grade bone sarcoma, but a meaningful proportion are secondary, arising after prior radiation, in Paget disease of bone, or at sites of bone infarct; a diagnosis of exclusion after ruling out osteosarcoma.
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed (Soft Tissue and Bone, 2020)

name: Adamantinomatous craniopharyngioma
potential_causes: Sporadic tumor with no established cause; the defining somatic CTNNB1 (beta-catenin) mutation is a driver rather than an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Anaplastic meningioma
aliases: Malignant meningioma; Meningioma CNS WHO grade 3
potential_causes: Most are sporadic, but prior therapeutic ionizing radiation is a well established risk factor for meningioma and neurofibromatosis type 2 (germline NF2 mutation) predisposes to meningiomas including higher grade lesions.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Angiocentric glioma
potential_causes: Rare benign sporadic glioma of childhood and young adults with no established cause; the characteristic MYB or MYB-QKI alteration is a somatic driver, not an inherited cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Astroblastoma, MN1-altered
aliases: Astroblastoma MN1-altered; High-grade neuroepithelial tumor with MN1 alteration
potential_causes: Rare sporadic tumor with no established cause; the defining MN1 gene rearrangement is a somatic alteration rather than a heritable or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Astrocytoma, isocitrate dehydrogenase-mutant
aliases: Astrocytoma, IDH-mutant; IDH-mutant astrocytoma; Diffuse astrocytoma IDH-mutant CNS WHO grade 2-4
potential_causes: Sporadic with no established environmental cause; the defining IDH1 or IDH2 mutation is a somatic driver, not an inherited cause. Prior therapeutic ionizing radiation is an established risk factor for gliomas, and Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53) and constitutional mismatch repair deficiency predispose to gliomas in a minority.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Atypical choroid plexus papilloma
potential_causes: Mostly sporadic with no established environmental cause; germline TP53 mutation (Li-Fraumeni syndrome) predisposes to choroid plexus tumors and should be considered, particularly in children, although the syndrome link is strongest for choroid plexus carcinoma.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Atypical meningioma
potential_causes: Most are sporadic, but prior therapeutic ionizing radiation is a well established risk factor and neurofibromatosis type 2 (germline NF2 mutation) predisposes to meningiomas including higher grade tumors.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Central nervous system choriocarcinoma
potential_causes: A CNS germ cell tumor that is usually sporadic, but Klinefelter syndrome and Down syndrome are established predisposing conditions for intracranial germ cell tumors.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Central nervous system embryonal carcinoma
potential_causes: A CNS germ cell tumor that is usually sporadic, but Klinefelter syndrome and Down syndrome are established predisposing conditions for intracranial germ cell tumors.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Central nervous system mixed germ cell tumor
aliases: Mixed germ cell tumor of the CNS
potential_causes: A CNS germ cell tumor that is usually sporadic, but Klinefelter syndrome and Down syndrome are established predisposing conditions for intracranial germ cell tumors.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Central nervous system teratoma
aliases: Intracranial teratoma; Immature teratoma; Teratoma with somatic-type malignancy
potential_causes: A CNS germ cell tumor that is usually sporadic, but Klinefelter syndrome and Down syndrome are established predisposing conditions for intracranial germ cell tumors.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Central nervous system yolk sac tumor
aliases: Endodermal sinus tumor; CNS yolk sac tumor
potential_causes: Most intracranial germ cell tumors are sporadic, but Klinefelter syndrome and Down syndrome are established predisposing conditions for CNS germ cell tumors including yolk sac tumor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Central neurocytoma
potential_causes: Sporadic tumor of the lateral ventricles with no established cause and no recognized hereditary syndrome or environmental risk factor.

name: Cerebellar liponeurocytoma
potential_causes: Rare sporadic cerebellar tumor of adults with no established cause and no recognized hereditary or environmental risk factor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Chordoid glioma
aliases: Chordoid glioma of the third ventricle
potential_causes: Rare sporadic tumor of the third ventricle with no established cause; the recurrent PRKCA D463H mutation is a somatic driver, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Choroid plexus carcinoma
potential_causes: Strongly linked to Li-Fraumeni syndrome from a germline TP53 mutation, especially in young children; germline TP53 alteration is found in a large proportion of pediatric cases and any child with this tumor warrants TP53 testing.

name: Desmoplastic myxoid tumor of the pineal region, SMARCB1-mutant
potential_causes: Very rare, recently described sporadic pineal tumor with no established cause; the SMARCB1 alteration reported to date is a somatic event rather than a proven inherited cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Diffuse glioneuronal tumor with oligodendroglioma-like features and nuclear clusters
potential_causes: Rare, recently described sporadic tumor with no established cause and no recognized hereditary or environmental risk factor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Diffuse hemispheric glioma, H3 G34-mutant
aliases: DHG H3 G34-mutant; H3 G34-mutant glioma
potential_causes: Sporadic high-grade glioma of adolescents and young adults with no established cause; the defining H3 G34R/V mutation is a somatic event rather than an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Diffuse leptomeningeal glioneuronal tumor
potential_causes: Rare sporadic tumor with no established cause; recurrent KIAA1549-BRAF fusion combined with 1p deletion is a somatic feature rather than an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Diffuse midline glioma, H3 K27-altered
aliases: DMG H3 K27-altered; Diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma; DIPG
potential_causes: Sporadic pediatric high-grade glioma with no established cause; the defining H3 K27M (or EZHIP) alteration is a somatic driver, not a heritable or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Diffuse pediatric-type high-grade glioma, H3-wildtype and IDH-wildtype
potential_causes: Largely sporadic with no established cause; a subset arises in the context of cancer predisposition syndromes such as constitutional mismatch repair deficiency, Li-Fraumeni syndrome, and neurofibromatosis type 1, and prior therapeutic radiation is a recognized risk factor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Esthesioneuroblastoma
potential_causes: No established cause; almost all cases are sporadic and no consistent risk factor is proven. Occupational exposure to wood dust, solvents, and heavy metals has been proposed but not confirmed, and there is no inherited predisposition.

name: Extraventricular neurocytoma
potential_causes: Rare sporadic tumor with no established cause and no recognized hereditary syndrome or environmental risk factor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Ganglioglioma
potential_causes: Sporadic glioneuronal tumor with no established cause; the frequent BRAF V600E mutation is a somatic driver rather than a heritable or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Germinoma
aliases: Central nervous system germinoma; Intracranial germinoma
potential_causes: Most intracranial germinomas are sporadic, but Klinefelter syndrome (sex chromosome aneuploidy with extra X) and Down syndrome are established predisposing conditions that raise the risk of CNS germ cell tumors.

name: Gliosarcoma
potential_causes: A variant of IDH-wildtype glioblastoma that is essentially always sporadic with no established cause; prior therapeutic cranial ionizing radiation is a recognized risk factor for high-grade gliomas generally, and rare cases arise after radiotherapy.

name: Hemangioblastoma
potential_causes: About one quarter arise in von Hippel-Lindau disease from a germline VHL tumor suppressor mutation, which is the only established genetic cause; the remaining sporadic tumors have no established cause.

name: High-grade astrocytoma with piloid features
potential_causes: Sporadic tumor with no established cause; recurrent MAPK pathway alterations, CDKN2A/B loss, and ATRX changes are somatic events, although rare cases arise on a background of neurofibromatosis type 1.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Infant-type hemispheric glioma
potential_causes: Sporadic infant glioma with no established cause; the recurrent receptor tyrosine kinase fusions (ALK, ROS1, NTRK, MET) are somatic drivers rather than inherited or environmental causes.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Myxoid glioneuronal tumor
potential_causes: Rare sporadic tumor with no established cause; the defining PDGFRA dinucleotide mutation is a somatic event rather than an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Myxopapillary ependymoma
potential_causes: Sporadic tumor of the filum terminale/conus with no established cause and no recognized hereditary or environmental risk factor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Oligodendroglioma, isocitrate dehydrogenase-mutant and 1p/19q-codeleted
potential_causes: Sporadic with no established cause; IDH mutation and 1p/19q codeletion are somatic defining events, not inherited causes. Prior therapeutic ionizing radiation is a recognized risk factor for gliomas in general.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Papillary craniopharyngioma
potential_causes: Sporadic tumor of adults with no established cause; the defining somatic BRAF V600E mutation is a driver rather than an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Papillary glioneuronal tumor
potential_causes: Rare sporadic glioneuronal tumor with no established cause; the defining PRKCA fusion (typically SLC44A1-PRKCA) is a somatic driver, not a heritable or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Papillary tumor of the pineal region
potential_causes: Rare sporadic tumor of the pineal region with no established cause and no recognized hereditary or environmental risk factor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Pilocytic astrocytoma
potential_causes: Most are sporadic with no known cause, but neurofibromatosis type 1 (germline NF1 mutation) strongly predisposes to pilocytic astrocytoma, particularly optic pathway glioma, in which roughly 15 to 20 percent of children with NF1 develop an optic pathway tumor.

name: Pineal parenchymal tumor of intermediate differentiation
potential_causes: Rare sporadic pineal tumor with no established cause and no recognized hereditary or environmental risk factor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Pleomorphic xanthoastrocytoma
potential_causes: Sporadic tumor with no established cause; the frequent BRAF V600E mutation and CDKN2A/B loss are somatic events, not heritable or environmental causes.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Posterior fossa ependymoma, group PFA
aliases: PFA ependymoma; Posterior fossa group A ependymoma
potential_causes: Sporadic infant and childhood ependymoma with no established cause; it is defined by a CpG island hypermethylation and EZHIP overexpression epigenetic phenotype (a somatic feature), not by an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Posterior fossa ependymoma, group PFB
aliases: PFB ependymoma; Posterior fossa group B ependymoma
potential_causes: Sporadic posterior fossa ependymoma of older children and adults with no established cause; it is defined by its methylation and cytogenetic profile rather than a heritable or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Primary diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of the central nervous system
aliases: Primary CNS lymphoma; PCNSL; Primary diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of the CNS
potential_causes: In immunodeficient patients (HIV/AIDS, transplant recipients, congenital immunodeficiency) it is caused by Epstein-Barr virus driven B-cell proliferation in the setting of immunosuppression; in immunocompetent patients it is largely sporadic with no established cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Rosette-forming glioneuronal tumor
potential_causes: Rare sporadic tumor, typically of the fourth ventricle, with no established cause; recurrent FGFR1 and PIK3CA/NF1 alterations are somatic events rather than inherited or environmental causes.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Solitary fibrous tumor of the central nervous system
potential_causes: Sporadic mesenchymal tumor with no established cause; the defining NAB2-STAT6 fusion is a somatic driver rather than an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Spinal ependymoma, MYCN-amplified
potential_causes: Rare aggressive spinal ependymoma with no established cause; the defining MYCN amplification is a somatic driver, not an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Subependymal giant cell astrocytoma
potential_causes: Occurs almost exclusively in tuberous sclerosis complex, an autosomal dominant disorder from germline TSC1 or TSC2 mutation, with biallelic TSC inactivation driving the tumor; it is therefore a syndrome-defining lesion rather than a sporadic tumor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Subependymoma
potential_causes: Benign, often incidental sporadic tumor with no established cause and no recognized hereditary or environmental risk factor.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Supratentorial ependymoma, YAP1 fusion-positive
potential_causes: Sporadic ependymoma of young children with no established cause; the defining YAP1 fusion is a somatic driver, not a heritable or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Supratentorial ependymoma, ZFTA fusion-positive
aliases: ST-EPN ZFTA fusion-positive; RELA fusion-positive ependymoma
potential_causes: Sporadic ependymoma with no established cause; the defining ZFTA (formerly C11orf95) fusion, most often with RELA, is a somatic driver rather than an inherited or environmental cause.
source: WHO CNS5 (2021)

name: Acute promyelocytic leukemia with PML-RARA fusion
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause; the PML::RARA fusion is a somatic event. A recognized subset is therapy-related, caused by prior treatment with topoisomerase II inhibitors (anthracyclines, mitoxantrone, etoposide) or alkylating agents.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Adult T-cell leukemia/lymphoma
potential_causes: Caused by chronic infection with human T-cell lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1), typically acquired in infancy through breastfeeding; HTLV-1 is regarded as the sole cause, with disease arising decades later in about 5 percent of carriers. It is endemic in Japan, the Caribbean, sub-Saharan Africa, and parts of South America.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Aggressive NK-cell leukemia
potential_causes: Strongly associated with Epstein-Barr virus, which is detected in nearly all cases, particularly in East Asian populations; EBV is regarded as central to its pathogenesis.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Aggressive systemic mastocytosis
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is driven by an acquired KIT D816V mutation, usually with additional myeloid mutations (SRSF2, ASXL1, RUNX1). These are somatic events, not external causes.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: ALK-positive histiocytosis
potential_causes: Cause is not an external exposure; it is defined by acquired ALK rearrangements (usually KIF5B::ALK), a somatic driver. No infectious, environmental, or hereditary cause is established.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Anaplastic large cell lymphoma, ALK-negative
aliases: ALK-negative ALCL; ALK- anaplastic large cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is a systemic T-cell lymphoma defined by acquired lesions (e.g., DUSP22 or TP63 rearrangements) with no confirmed external cause. It is distinct from the breast implant-associated form.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Anaplastic large cell lymphoma, ALK-positive
aliases: ALK-positive ALCL; ALK+ anaplastic large cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is defined by an acquired ALK rearrangement (usually NPM1::ALK), a somatic driver rather than an external cause. No confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Atypical chronic myeloid leukemia
potential_causes: Cause is generally not established; it is a BCR::ABL1-negative myelodysplastic/myeloproliferative neoplasm driven by acquired mutations (SETBP1, ETNK1, ASXL1). A subset may relate to prior cytotoxic therapy or benzene exposure, but most cases are idiopathic.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: B-prolymphocytic leukemia
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this rare B-cell leukemia (now largely reclassified within other entities) is idiopathic with no confirmed infectious, environmental, or hereditary cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm
potential_causes: No established environmental, infectious, or hereditary cause; occurs mainly in older adults and is defined by recurrent deletions (RB1, CDKN2A, TP53). A minority arise after immunosuppression or alongside other myeloid neoplasms.

name: Bone marrow mastocytosis
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is a clonal mast cell disorder driven by an acquired KIT D816V mutation. This somatic driver is not an external cause, and no environmental etiology is confirmed.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Breast implant-associated anaplastic large cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Caused by textured-surface breast implants placed for reconstruction or cosmesis; an epidemiologic causal relationship is established and the leading mechanism is chronic inflammation from bacterial biofilm on the textured shell in predisposed individuals. Smooth implants are not implicated.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Burkitt lymphoma
potential_causes: The endemic (African) form is caused by Epstein-Barr virus with holoendemic Plasmodium falciparum malaria as a cofactor; the immunodeficiency-associated form is linked to HIV infection. The sporadic form has no established cause and is EBV-positive in only a minority.

name: Chronic eosinophilic leukemia
aliases: CEL; chronic eosinophilic leukaemia, not otherwise specified
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is a clonal myeloid proliferation defined by acquired genetic lesions, distinct from the tyrosine kinase fusion driven myeloid neoplasms. No external etiologic factor is proven.

name: Chronic myeloid leukemia in blast phase
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it represents progression of chronic myeloid leukemia, which is defined by the acquired BCR::ABL1 fusion (Philadelphia chromosome). High-dose ionizing radiation is the only proven environmental risk factor for the underlying CML; the fusion itself is a somatic event.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Chronic myelomonocytic leukemia
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with no identified cause; established myeloid leukemia risk factors (benzene exposure, cigarette smoking, prior cytotoxic chemotherapy or radiation) contribute to a subset, and it is a clonal disorder of older adults driven by acquired mutations such as TET2, SRSF2, and ASXL1.

name: Chronic neutrophilic leukemia
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is a rare myeloproliferative neoplasm driven in most cases by acquired activating CSF3R mutations (T618I), a somatic event rather than an external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Early T-cell precursor lymphoblastic leukemia/lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this immature T-lineage subtype is defined by a distinctive immunophenotype and acquired mutations (FLT3, RAS pathway, DNMT3A). No confirmed external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Enteropathy-associated T-cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Caused by celiac disease, arising from aberrant intraepithelial T lymphocytes in patients with (often refractory) gluten-sensitive enteropathy. Poor adherence to a gluten-free diet, HLA-DQ2 homozygosity, and late celiac diagnosis are recognized risk factors.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Erdheim-Chester disease
potential_causes: Cause is not an external exposure; it is a clonal histiocytic neoplasm driven by somatic MAPK/ERK pathway mutations, most often BRAF V600E. No infectious, environmental, or hereditary cause is established.

name: Essential thrombocythemia
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is a clonal myeloproliferative neoplasm driven by acquired JAK2, CALR, or MPL mutations. These are somatic events, not external causes.

name: Extranodal NK/T-cell lymphoma, nasal type
potential_causes: Caused by Epstein-Barr virus infection of the neoplastic NK or T cells in genetically susceptible individuals; EBV is present in essentially all cases and is central to pathogenesis. It is most common in East Asian and Latin American populations.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Extraosseous plasmacytoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this localized extramedullary clonal plasma cell tumor, most often in the upper aerodigestive tract, is idiopathic with no confirmed external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Follicular dendritic cell sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause is generally not established; most are sporadic. A distinct subset, the inflammatory pseudotumor-like variant arising in liver and spleen, is caused by Epstein-Barr virus.

name: Hairy cell leukemia
potential_causes: Cause is not established; the near-universal BRAF V600E mutation is a somatic driver, not an external etiology. Some studies report weak associations with prior farming, pesticide, or radiation exposure, but no risk factor is confirmed.

name: Hepatosplenic T cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Most cases arise de novo without an identified cause, but roughly 20 percent occur in the setting of chronic immunosuppression or immune dysregulation, notably long-term thiopurine and anti-TNF therapy for inflammatory bowel disease and prior solid organ transplantation.

name: Histiocytic sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause is generally not established; it is a rare malignant histiocytic neoplasm often driven by MAPK pathway mutations. A subset is transdifferentiated (clonally related) from a preceding B-cell lymphoma/leukemia or from germ cell tumors, but no external cause is confirmed.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Idiopathic multicentric Castleman disease
potential_causes: Cause is not established (hence idiopathic); it is an HHV-8-negative systemic inflammatory disorder driven by an unexplained cytokine storm, typically interleukin-6. The source of the hypercytokinemia and any triggering factor remain unknown.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Immunoglobulin light chain amyloidosis
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it results from a clonal plasma cell (or, less often, B-cell) proliferation producing misfolded free light chains that deposit as amyloid. The underlying clone is driven by acquired lesions; no external cause is confirmed.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Indolent systemic mastocytosis
potential_causes: Cause is not established; about 95 percent are driven by an acquired KIT D816V gain-of-function mutation, a somatic event, not an external exposure. Rare familial forms exist but the vast majority are sporadic.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Indolent T-cell lymphoma of the gastrointestinal tract
aliases: indolent clonal T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder of the gastrointestinal tract
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this indolent clonal T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder of the gut is idiopathic, sometimes with chronic mucosal inflammation, and is driven by acquired lesions (e.g., STAT3::JAK2 fusion, JAK/STAT pathway mutations). No confirmed external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Interdigitating dendritic cell sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause is generally not established; this rare dendritic cell neoplasm is idiopathic. A minority are clonally related to a preceding low-grade B-cell lymphoma (transdifferentiation), but no external cause is confirmed.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Intravascular large B-cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this rare large B-cell lymphoma growing within blood vessels is idiopathic, driven by acquired lesions (frequent MYD88 and CD79B mutations). No confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia
potential_causes: Cause is not an external exposure; it is driven by acquired or, in a subset, germline RAS pathway mutations. Children with neurofibromatosis type 1 and with Noonan syndrome (germline PTPN11) are predisposed, but there is no confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Juvenile xanthogranuloma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this histiocytic proliferation of infants and children is usually benign and idiopathic, with a subset harboring MAPK pathway mutations. An association with neurofibromatosis type 1 and juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia is recognized in a minority.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: KSHV/HHV8-associated multicentric Castleman disease
potential_causes: Caused by uncontrolled infection with Kaposi sarcoma-associated herpesvirus (KSHV/HHV-8); it is most often diagnosed in people with HIV infection or other immunosuppression, in whom impaired control of HHV-8 drives the lymphoproliferation.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Langerhans cell histiocytosis
potential_causes: Now recognized as a clonal neoplasm driven by somatic MAPK pathway mutations (BRAF V600E in about half of cases); these are acquired drivers not external causes. The pulmonary adult form is a distinct entity strongly caused by cigarette (and marijuana) smoking.

name: Langerhans cell sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this rare high-grade Langerhans cell neoplasm is driven by MAPK pathway mutations (including BRAF V600E) and may arise de novo or from progression of Langerhans cell histiocytosis. No confirmed external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Mantle cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; the defining t(11;14) CCND1 rearrangement is a somatic event. No confirmed infectious, environmental, or hereditary cause, though it is a clonal B-cell malignancy of older adults.

name: Mast cell leukemia
potential_causes: Cause is not established; like other mastocytoses it is driven by an acquired activating KIT mutation (usually D816V), a somatic event rather than an external cause. No environmental or infectious cause is proven.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Mast cell sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this exceedingly rare localized malignant mast cell tumor is idiopathic, with variable KIT mutation status and no confirmed external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Mixed phenotype acute leukemia
aliases: MPAL; acute leukemia of ambiguous lineage
potential_causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause; some are therapy-related after prior cytotoxic chemotherapy or radiation. It is defined by acquired genetic lesions and lineage-ambiguous immunophenotype.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Monomorphic epitheliotropic intestinal T-cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; unlike EATL it is NOT associated with celiac disease. It arises from intestinal intraepithelial T lymphocytes, is more common in Asian and Hispanic populations, and is driven by acquired mutations (e.g., SETD2, STAT5B).
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Mycosis fungoides
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this cutaneous T-cell lymphoma is largely idiopathic. Proposed but unproven contributors include chronic antigen stimulation and environmental or occupational exposures; no infectious agent is confirmed.

name: Myeloid/lymphoid neoplasm with eosinophilia and ETV6::ABL1 fusion
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is defined by an acquired ETV6::ABL1 fusion, a somatic tyrosine kinase fusion, not an external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Myeloid/lymphoid neoplasm with eosinophilia and FGFR1 rearrangement
aliases: MLN-eo with FGFR1 rearrangement; 8p11 myeloproliferative syndrome
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is defined by an acquired FGFR1 rearrangement, a somatic tyrosine kinase fusion, not an external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Myeloid/lymphoid neoplasm with eosinophilia and FLT3 rearrangement
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is defined by an acquired FLT3 rearrangement, a somatic tyrosine kinase fusion, not an external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Myeloid/lymphoid neoplasm with eosinophilia and JAK2 rearrangement
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is defined by an acquired JAK2 rearrangement (e.g., PCM1::JAK2), a somatic fusion, not an external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Myeloid/lymphoid neoplasm with eosinophilia and PDGFRA rearrangement
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is defined by an acquired PDGFRA rearrangement (usually the FIP1L1::PDGFRA fusion), a somatic driver rather than an external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Myeloid/lymphoid neoplasm with eosinophilia and PDGFRB rearrangement
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is defined by an acquired PDGFRB rearrangement, a somatic tyrosine kinase fusion, not an external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: NK-cell large granular lymphocytic leukemia
aliases: NK-LGLL; chronic lymphoproliferative disorder of NK cells
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this chronic NK-cell lymphoproliferation is idiopathic, associated with chronic immune activation and in some series with prior Epstein-Barr virus exposure, but no confirmed causal agent.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Nodal T-follicular helper cell lymphoma, angioimmunoblastic type
aliases: angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma; AITL; nodal TFH cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it arises from follicular helper T cells and is driven by acquired mutations (TET2, DNMT3A, IDH2, RHOA G17V). Epstein-Barr virus positive bystander B cells are characteristically present but EBV is not the neoplastic driver.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Plasma cell leukemia
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is an aggressive clonal plasma cell malignancy, arising de novo or evolving from multiple myeloma, driven by acquired cytogenetic abnormalities. No confirmed external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Plasmablastic lymphoma
potential_causes: Strongly associated with immunodeficiency, especially HIV/AIDS and iatrogenic immunosuppression after solid organ transplant, and Epstein-Barr virus is detected in most cases; MYC rearrangement is a frequent cofactor. The full pathogenesis remains incompletely defined.

name: Polycythemia vera
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is a clonal myeloproliferative neoplasm caused by an acquired JAK2 mutation (V617F in most cases), which is a somatic driver rather than an external cause. No confirmed environmental etiology.

name: Primary cutaneous CD30-positive T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder
aliases: primary cutaneous anaplastic large cell lymphoma; lymphomatoid papulosis
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this spectrum (lymphomatoid papulosis and primary cutaneous anaplastic large cell lymphoma) is idiopathic. It is defined by CD30 expression and acquired lesions, with no confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Primary cutaneous CD8-positive aggressive epidermotropic cytotoxic T-cell lymphoma
aliases: primary cutaneous aggressive epidermotropic CD8-positive cytotoxic T-cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this rare aggressive cutaneous cytotoxic T-cell lymphoma is idiopathic with no confirmed external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Primary cutaneous diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, leg type
aliases: PCDLBCL-LT; primary cutaneous diffuse large B-cell lymphoma leg type
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this aggressive cutaneous B-cell lymphoma of elderly patients is driven by acquired lesions (MYD88 L265P, BCL2/MYC) with no confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Primary cutaneous follicle center lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is generally not established; it is an indolent cutaneous B-cell lymphoma of follicle center cells. Borrelia burgdorferi has been proposed as a cofactor in some European cases but the association is not confirmed, and most cases are idiopathic.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Primary cutaneous gamma/delta T-cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this aggressive cutaneous lymphoma of gamma/delta T cells is idiopathic with no confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Primary cutaneous marginal zone lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is generally not established; it is an indolent cutaneous B-cell lymphoma. Chronic antigenic stimulation, including Borrelia burgdorferi infection in some European cohorts, has been implicated as a cofactor but is not confirmed, and most cases are idiopathic.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Primary effusion lymphoma
potential_causes: Caused by Kaposi sarcoma-associated herpesvirus (KSHV/HHV-8), which is necessary for the disease; it occurs almost exclusively in the setting of severe immunosuppression, most often advanced HIV/AIDS, and HIV-positive cases are frequently co-infected with Epstein-Barr virus.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Primary mediastinal large B-cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this thymic B-cell lymphoma of young adults is driven by acquired lesions (9p24/JAK2 and PD-L1/PD-L2 amplification, NF-kB pathway alterations). No confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Primary myelofibrosis
potential_causes: Cause is generally not established; it is a clonal myeloproliferative neoplasm driven by acquired JAK2 V617F, CALR, or MPL mutations, which are somatic events. Ionizing radiation and benzene exposure are recognized but uncommon contributors.

name: Rosai-Dorfman disease
aliases: RDD; sinus histiocytosis with massive lymphadenopathy
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is regarded as idiopathic, with a subset harboring somatic MAPK pathway (KRAS, MAP2K1) mutations and rare familial forms tied to SLC29A3 mutations. No confirmed infectious or environmental cause.

name: Sezary syndrome
potential_causes: Cause is not established; the leukemic erythrodermic form of cutaneous T-cell lymphoma is idiopathic with no confirmed infectious or environmental cause.

name: Smoldering systemic mastocytosis
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is a clonal mast cell disorder driven by an acquired KIT D816V mutation, a somatic driver rather than an external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Solitary plasmacytoma of bone
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is a localized clonal plasma cell tumor and part of the plasma cell neoplasm spectrum. No confirmed infectious, environmental, or hereditary cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Splenic B-cell lymphoma/leukemia with prominent nucleoli
aliases: SBLPN; hairy cell leukemia variant; HCL-variant
potential_causes: Cause is not established; this rare splenic small B-cell neoplasm (formerly splenic diffuse red pulp lymphoma / hairy cell leukemia variant spectrum) is idiopathic with no confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Subcutaneous panniculitis-like T-cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is idiopathic and frequently associated with autoimmunity. A subset arises in patients with germline HAVCR2 (TIM-3) mutations that predispose to an associated hemophagocytic syndrome, but most cases have no external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Systemic mastocytosis with an associated hematologic neoplasm
potential_causes: Cause is not established; the mast cell clone and the accompanying myeloid neoplasm share acquired mutations (KIT D816V plus SRSF2/ASXL1/RUNX1). These are somatic drivers, not external causes.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: T-cell large granular lymphocytic leukemia
aliases: T-LGLL; T-cell large granular lymphocytic leukaemia
potential_causes: Cause is not established, but it is strongly linked to chronic immune stimulation and autoimmune disease, notably rheumatoid arthritis (overlapping with Felty syndrome), and is driven by acquired activating STAT3 mutations. No external etiologic agent is proven.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: T-lymphoblastic leukemia/lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause is generally not established; it is driven by acquired mutations (e.g., NOTCH1) and is a sporadic malignancy. Prior chemotherapy or radiation and inherited cancer predisposition syndromes contribute to a minority, but most cases are idiopathic.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: T-prolymphocytic leukemia
potential_causes: Cause is not established; it is driven by acquired chromosome 14 inversions/translocations activating TCL1 and by ATM inactivation. Patients with the hereditary ATM-deficiency disorder ataxia-telangiectasia are predisposed, but sporadic cases have no external cause.
source: WHO-HAEM5 2022

name: Acute promyelocytic leukemia
potential_causes: Cause largely not established; defined by the t(15;17) PML::RARA fusion and usually arises de novo, though therapy-related cases occur after prior exposure to topoisomerase II inhibitor chemotherapy.

name: Adrenal neuroblastoma
potential_causes: Cause largely not established; most are sporadic embryonal tumors of neural crest origin, with a minority hereditary due to germline ALK mutations or PHOX2B mutations (the latter in congenital central hypoventilation and Hirschsprung syndrome).

name: Anal cancer
aliases: Anal squamous cell carcinoma; Squamous cell carcinoma of the anus; Anal canal SCC
potential_causes: Caused predominantly by persistent high-risk human papillomavirus (especially HPV-16) infection; risk is amplified by HIV infection, immunosuppression, receptive anal intercourse, and smoking. Precursor lesion is anal intraepithelial neoplasia.

name: Anaplastic large cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause largely not established for systemic ALK-positive and ALK-negative forms; the breast implant-associated variant is a clear exception, causally linked to textured breast implants with chronic inflammation.

name: Angioimmunoblastic T cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; frequently associated with Epstein-Barr virus in the tumor microenvironment and driven by recurrent T follicular helper cell mutations (TET2, DNMT3A, RHOA, IDH2), but no confirmed etiologic cause. Largely sporadic.

name: Appendiceal cancer
potential_causes: Cause not established; most appendiceal tumors, including mucinous neoplasms and appendiceal neuroendocrine tumors, arise sporadically with no confirmed environmental or hereditary etiology.

name: Carcinoma of unknown primary
potential_causes: Cause not established by definition; it is metastatic carcinoma without an identifiable primary site, so no single etiology applies, though it reflects underlying carcinomas whose own risk factors (smoking, alcohol, and others) may be relevant.

name: Cholangiocarcinoma
aliases: Bile duct cancer; Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma; iCCA; Intrahepatic bile duct carcinoma
potential_causes: Chronic biliary inflammation is the main driver: primary sclerosing cholangitis, liver fluke infection (Opisthorchis viverrini, Clonorchis sinensis), hepatolithiasis, choledochal cysts, chronic hepatitis B or C, cirrhosis, and thorotrast exposure. Precursor lesions include biliary intraepithelial neoplasia and intraductal papillary neoplasm of the bile duct.

name: Chondrosarcoma
potential_causes: Most conventional cases are sporadic, but a subset arises from precursor cartilage lesions such as enchondromas (Ollier disease, Maffucci syndrome) and osteochondromas (hereditary multiple exostoses due to EXT1/EXT2 mutations).

name: Chordoma
potential_causes: Arises from notochordal remnants along the axial skeleton; a benign notochordal cell tumor is a precursor, and germline duplication of the T (brachyury/TBXT) gene predisposes in familial chordoma. Most cases are otherwise sporadic.

name: Clear cell sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; this melanocytic soft tissue sarcoma is defined by an EWSR1::ATF1 fusion but has no known inherited or environmental cause, and is sporadic.

name: Collecting duct carcinoma of the kidney
potential_causes: Cause not established; this aggressive renal cancer arises from the distal collecting duct with no confirmed inherited or environmental etiology, and is generally sporadic.

name: Craniopharyngioma
potential_causes: Cause not established; adamantinomatous type arises from embryonic Rathke pouch epithelial remnants with CTNNB1 mutations, and papillary type carries BRAF V600E mutations, but neither has a known inherited or environmental cause. Sporadic.

name: Cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma
potential_causes: Chronic cumulative ultraviolet (sun) exposure is the dominant cause; other factors include immunosuppression (organ transplant, HIV), human papillomavirus, chronic wounds and scars (Marjolin ulcer), arsenic and radiation exposure, and genodermatoses such as xeroderma pigmentosum and epidermolysis bullosa.

name: Desmoid tumor
potential_causes: Predisposed by germline APC mutations in familial adenomatous polyposis (Gardner syndrome); sporadic desmoids are driven by CTNNB1 (beta-catenin) mutations and are associated with prior trauma or surgery, pregnancy, and estrogen exposure.

name: Diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma
potential_causes: Cause not established; this pediatric brainstem glioma is defined by histone H3 K27 mutations and has no known inherited or environmental cause. Sporadic.

name: Endometrial stromal sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; defined by recurrent gene fusions (for example JAZF1::SUZ12) but has no confirmed inherited or environmental cause, and tamoxifen use and unopposed estrogen have been weakly implicated. Generally sporadic.

name: Ependymoma
potential_causes: Mostly sporadic; the main recognized hereditary predisposition is neurofibromatosis type 2 (germline NF2), which is associated with spinal ependymomas. No established environmental cause.

name: Extragonadal germ cell tumor
potential_causes: Predisposing conditions include Klinefelter syndrome (associated with mediastinal germ cell tumors) and gonadal dysgenesis; these arise from misplaced primordial germ cells along the midline and are otherwise largely of unestablished cause.

name: Extranodal NK/T cell lymphoma
potential_causes: Causally associated with Epstein-Barr virus infection, which is present in essentially all cases; it is most common in East Asian and Latin American populations.

name: Fibrosarcoma
potential_causes: Cause largely not established; adult fibrosarcoma is usually sporadic, and infantile fibrosarcoma is defined by an ETV6::NTRK3 fusion. Prior radiation is a recognized cause of some secondary fibrosarcomas.

name: Gallbladder cancer
potential_causes: Strongly linked to chronic inflammation from gallstones (cholelithiasis), porcelain (calcified) gallbladder, chronic Salmonella typhi (typhoid) carriage, and gallbladder polyps; anomalous pancreaticobiliary duct junction and obesity are additional risk factors.

name: Gastrointestinal neuroendocrine tumor
potential_causes: Most are sporadic with no established cause, but recognized predisposing conditions include multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1), and for gastric types, chronic atrophic gastritis with hypergastrinemia (autoimmune or Zollinger-Ellison syndrome).

name: Gestational trophoblastic disease
potential_causes: Arises from abnormal fertilization producing hydatidiform mole (complete moles from androgenetic diploidy, partial moles from triploid dispermic fertilization); a prior molar pregnancy and extremes of maternal age are risk factors, and rare familial recurrent hydatidiform mole is caused by NLRP7 or KHDC3L mutations.

name: Glioblastoma
potential_causes: Cause not established for the vast majority; the only firmly established environmental risk factor is prior ionizing radiation to the head, and rare cases occur in hereditary syndromes (Li-Fraumeni, Lynch/constitutional mismatch repair deficiency, neurofibromatosis). Otherwise sporadic.

name: Granulosa cell tumor of the ovary
potential_causes: Cause not established; adult type is characterized by a somatic FOXL2 mutation but has no known inherited or environmental cause, and juvenile type can occur in enchondromatosis syndromes (Ollier disease, Maffucci syndrome). Generally sporadic.

name: Hurthle cell thyroid carcinoma
aliases: Oncocytic thyroid carcinoma; Hurthle cell carcinoma; oxyphilic follicular carcinoma
potential_causes: Ionizing radiation exposure to the head and neck, especially in childhood, is an established risk factor; chronic autoimmune thyroiditis (Hashimoto) and iodine deficiency are associated, and rare familial nonmedullary thyroid cancer clustering occurs, but most cases are sporadic.

name: Inflammatory breast cancer
potential_causes: Cause not established; it is an aggressive clinical presentation of breast carcinoma without a specific etiologic factor, though obesity and general breast cancer risk factors apply and it is usually sporadic.

name: Large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma
potential_causes: When pulmonary, it is strongly caused by tobacco smoking, similar to small cell lung cancer; large cell neuroendocrine carcinomas at other sites are largely of unestablished cause.

name: Liposarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; liposarcomas are generally sporadic soft tissue tumors defined by characteristic genetic changes (for example MDM2 amplification) without a confirmed inherited or environmental etiology.

name: Low grade serous ovarian carcinoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; arises from serous borderline (atypical proliferative) tumor as a precursor lesion but has no confirmed inherited or environmental etiologic cause, and is generally sporadic.

name: Male breast cancer
potential_causes: Predisposed by germline BRCA2 (and BRCA1) mutations, Klinefelter syndrome, conditions of estrogen excess or androgen deficiency (obesity, cirrhosis, testicular disorders), prior chest radiation, and family history.

name: Malignant mesothelioma
aliases: Pleural mesothelioma; malignant pleural mesothelioma; diffuse pleural mesothelioma; epithelioid mesothelioma; sarcomatoid mesothelioma; biphasic mesothelioma
potential_causes: Asbestos exposure is the established cause in the great majority of cases, with a long latency of decades; erionite fiber exposure and prior chest radiation are additional causes, and germline BAP1 mutation predisposes.

name: Medulloblastoma
potential_causes: Mostly sporadic, but predisposed by hereditary syndromes: Gorlin (nevoid basal cell carcinoma) syndrome and other SHH-pathway germline defects for SHH-activated tumors, and germline APC (Turcot syndrome) for WNT-activated tumors, as well as Li-Fraumeni syndrome.

name: Meningioma
