potential_causes: Prior ionizing radiation to the head is an established cause; neurofibromatosis type 2 (germline NF2) predisposes to multiple meningiomas, and hormonal factors are associated. Many cases are otherwise sporadic.

name: Multicentric Castleman disease
potential_causes: A large fraction is caused by uncontrolled human herpesvirus-8 (HHV-8) infection, typically in the setting of HIV or immunosuppression; the remaining HHV-8-negative idiopathic form has an unknown cause driven by IL-6-mediated cytokine storm.

name: Nonseminomatous germ cell tumor
potential_causes: Shares testicular germ cell tumor risk factors, notably cryptorchidism, family or personal history, and testicular dysgenesis; develops from germ cell neoplasia in situ.

name: Oligodendroglioma
potential_causes: Cause not established; defined by IDH mutation and 1p/19q codeletion but with no confirmed inherited or environmental cause. Prior brain irradiation is a general risk factor for gliomas; otherwise sporadic.

name: Osteosarcoma
potential_causes: Predisposed by hereditary cancer syndromes (hereditary retinoblastoma with germline RB1, Li-Fraumeni syndrome with germline TP53, Rothmund-Thomson, Werner, Bloom syndromes), prior therapeutic radiation, and Paget disease of bone in older adults.

name: Ovarian germ cell tumor
potential_causes: Cause largely not established; these tumors arise from primordial germ cells and are usually sporadic, with gonadal dysgenesis (dysgenetic gonads, especially with Y chromosome material) being a recognized predisposing condition for some subtypes.

name: Papillary renal cell carcinoma
potential_causes: Hereditary forms have defined causes: hereditary papillary RCC from germline MET mutations (type 1) and hereditary leiomyomatosis and RCC from germline fumarate hydratase mutations (type 2); acquired cystic kidney disease and end-stage renal disease are acquired risk factors, but most cases are sporadic.

name: PEComa
potential_causes: A subset arises in the tuberous sclerosis complex (germline TSC1/TSC2 mutations); most sporadic PEComas have no established inherited or environmental cause.

name: Penile cancer
potential_causes: Caused by high-risk HPV infection in a large fraction of cases; other risk factors include phimosis with chronic inflammation, lack of neonatal circumcision, lichen sclerosus, smoking, and poor hygiene. Precursor is penile intraepithelial neoplasia.

name: Primary central nervous system lymphoma
potential_causes: Severe immunosuppression is the main established cause, especially HIV/AIDS (with Epstein-Barr virus driving the tumor) and iatrogenic immunosuppression after transplantation; immunocompetent cases are otherwise of unestablished cause.

name: Pulmonary carcinoid tumor
potential_causes: Cause largely not established; most pulmonary carcinoids are not linked to smoking, and a minority occur in the setting of multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (germline MEN1 mutation) or arise from diffuse idiopathic pulmonary neuroendocrine cell hyperplasia as a precursor.

name: Rhabdomyosarcoma
potential_causes: Mostly sporadic, but predisposed by hereditary syndromes including Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53), neurofibromatosis type 1, DICER1 syndrome, Costello syndrome, and Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome.

name: Sacrococcygeal teratoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; this congenital germ cell tumor arises from pluripotent cells near the coccyx (Hensen node remnants) and is generally sporadic, with rare familial presacral teratoma linked to Currarino syndrome (MNX1 mutation).

name: Salivary gland carcinoma
potential_causes: Prior ionizing radiation to the head and neck is the best-established risk factor; some subtypes are associated with defined genetic fusions rather than external causes, and most cases are otherwise sporadic.

name: Sarcomatoid carcinoma
potential_causes: Cause not established as a distinct entity; it represents dedifferentiation of an underlying epithelial carcinoma (for example of lung, kidney, or urothelium), so its risk factors are those of the parent carcinoma rather than a specific separate cause.

name: Seminoma
potential_causes: Same predisposing factors as other testicular germ cell tumors, chiefly cryptorchidism, prior testicular tumor, family history, and testicular dysgenesis; arises from germ cell neoplasia in situ.

name: Small bowel adenocarcinoma
potential_causes: Predisposed by inherited syndromes (Lynch syndrome, familial adenomatous polyposis, Peutz-Jeghers syndrome) and by chronic small bowel inflammation, notably Crohn disease and celiac disease.

name: Small cell lung cancer
potential_causes: Overwhelmingly caused by tobacco smoking; radon exposure and occupational carcinogens are additional causes. It very rarely occurs in never-smokers.

name: Solitary fibrous tumor
potential_causes: Cause not established; defined by a NAB2::STAT6 fusion but without a confirmed inherited or environmental etiologic factor. Generally sporadic.

name: Spindle cell sarcoma
potential_causes: Cause not established; spindle cell sarcoma is a morphologic category encompassing several entities with no single confirmed etiology, and most are sporadic (some secondary to prior radiation).

name: Systemic mastocytosis
potential_causes: Cause not established; the great majority carry an acquired somatic KIT D816V mutation but there is no confirmed inherited or environmental cause, and rare familial cases with germline KIT variants exist. Largely sporadic.

name: T cell prolymphocytic leukemia
potential_causes: Cause largely not established; strongly associated with inversion/translocation of the TCL1 locus, and markedly increased in ataxia-telangiectasia (germline ATM mutation), but sporadic cases have no known external cause.

name: Testicular germ cell tumor
potential_causes: Predisposed by cryptorchidism (undescended testis), personal or family history of testicular cancer, testicular dysgenesis, infertility, and Klinefelter syndrome; germ cell neoplasia in situ is the precursor lesion.

name: Translocation renal cell carcinoma
potential_causes: Prior exposure to cytotoxic chemotherapy in childhood is the only established risk factor; the tumors are driven by MiT family (TFE3/TFEB) gene fusions, but most arise sporadically without a known external cause.

name: Urachal carcinoma
potential_causes: Arises from persistent embryonic urachal remnants at the bladder dome; no definitive environmental or hereditary risk factors are established, and it is otherwise considered sporadic.

name: Urethral cancer
potential_causes: Associated with high-risk HPV infection (especially distal urethral squamous cancers), chronic urethral inflammation, urethral strictures, and recurrent urinary tract infections; a history of bladder cancer also raises risk.

name: Vaginal cancer
potential_causes: Most squamous vaginal cancers are caused by persistent high-risk human papillomavirus infection; clear cell adenocarcinoma of the vagina is linked to in utero diethylstilbestrol (DES) exposure. Precursor is vaginal intraepithelial neoplasia.

name: Vulvar cancer
aliases: Vulvar squamous cell carcinoma; squamous cell carcinoma of the vulva
potential_causes: Two pathways: high-risk HPV infection (with usual-type vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia) and HPV-independent disease arising in chronic inflammatory dermatoses such as lichen sclerosus with differentiated vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia; smoking and immunosuppression add risk.

name: Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia
potential_causes: Cause not established; nearly all cases carry a somatic MYD88 L265P mutation and it is preceded by IgM monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance, with familial clustering suggesting inherited predisposition, but no confirmed environmental cause. Largely sporadic.

name: Yolk sac tumor
potential_causes: Cause largely not established; this germ cell tumor arises from primordial germ cells and is usually sporadic, with gonadal dysgenesis being a predisposing condition for gonadal cases.
